markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Red-Rib Gill Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1447)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mask is made from the gills of the red-rib salamander, an amphibious creature unique to Terwa Lake. Once activated, the mask intensifies the odors of gaseous toxins, allowing you to automatically detect toxic fumes within 30 feet and their approximate source (making the source [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) instead of [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41)). You can't wear other masks while you're wearing a _red-rib gill mask_.

 After Activating the mask, you also gain the listed item bonus to Fortitude saves against [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) effects; this bonus is increased against the deadly volcanic gas known around Terwa Lake as Dead Man's Breath, and possibly against other similar volcanic gases at the GM's discretion.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Red-Rib Gill Mask (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 76

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +1, or +2 against Dead Man's Breath, and the duration is 10 minutes.

---

The item bonus is +2, or +3 against Dead Man's Breath, and the duration is 1 hour.

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

---

The item bonus is +3, or +4 against Dead Man's Breath, and the duration is 8 hours.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Deflecting Branch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1448)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 75

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greatclub](/Weapons.aspx?ID=22)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This massive branch has been cut into a general club shape but still bears several knots and has a number of runes carved along its length. The deflecting branch is a _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greatclub](/Weapons.aspx?ID=22)_. On a critical hit, you knock the target [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** You are the target of a physical ranged attack; **Requirements** You're aware of the attack and are not [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against it; **Effect** You gain a +4 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses, you have knocked it away. You can't use this ability to deflect unusually massive ranged projectiles (such as boulders or ballista bolts).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You knock away a physical ranged attack by using the _deflecting branch's_ other activation;  **Effect** You swing the club and reflect the oncoming attack. The attack is redirected toward a creature within 60 feet. Make a ranged attack against the creature using your highest proficiency with a ranged weapon. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Deflecting Branch

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands **Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 76 **Price** 6,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10) --- Each of these golden spears was forged in the days of old Mzali, when the sun kings ruled the city. The majority of these weapons are lost, most likely buried with warriors in tombs and temples sealed by [Walkena's](/Deities.aspx?ID=159) decrees. Walkena retains a single golden blade, which he grants to his Master of Spears. This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10)_ is coated in brilliant gold, and beautiful engravings run along the shaft, depicting the rising and setting of the sun against Mzali's skyline. The spear glows with the effects of 4th-level [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171). You can suppress or resume this light by using an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. When you hit a creature with the spear, it takes 1d6 additional fire damage. When you critically hit a creature with the spear, the creature takes 1d8 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 1d8 persistent bleed damage as its blood boils away. Unlike normal when taking two types of persistent damage, a creature needs only a single successful flat check to remove both the fire and bleed damage. **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically hit a creature with the weapon; **Effect** You call upon the spear's light to guide the creature away from violence. The creature must choose one of the following options:
  • The creature surrenders, immediately receiving the critical failure effects of [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=31) and [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=213).
  • The weapon casts 7th-level [_blindness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=26) on the creature with a DC of 34.
Regardless of the choice, the spear loses its ability to glow with light until your next daily preparations. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The spear casts 7th-level [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=98) on you, choosing fire as the form's trait. The spear loses its additional fire damage until your next daily preparations.
name: Golden Blade of Mzali

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Midday Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1450)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hooded lantern is made from brilliant, reinforced gold engraved with the image of the sun high in the sky. The lantern uses oil, as a standard lantern, except that any light the lantern emits is magical and similar to sunlight. The lantern doesn't emit dim light, instead only emitting bright light to its normal radius (20 feet for typical lights when using oil). This bright light is close enough to sunlight to open temple doors that require sunlight or similar light, but it doesn't shine direct sunlight, so it doesn't trigger effects such as a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97)'s sunlight weakness.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the lantern and unleash a blast of powerful sunlight. The blast has the effects of 2nd-level [_scorching ray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=992) using a spell attack roll of +14, with its effect determined by the number of actions you used to

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hooded lantern is made from brilliant, reinforced gold engraved with the image of the sun high in the sky. The lantern uses oil, as a standard lantern, except that any light the lantern emits is magical and similar to sunlight. The lantern doesn't emit dim light, instead only emitting bright light to its normal radius (20 feet for typical lights when using oil). This bright light is close enough to sunlight to open temple doors that require sunlight or similar light, but it doesn't shine direct sunlight, so it doesn't trigger effects such as a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97)'s sunlight weakness.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the lantern and unleash a blast of powerful sunlight. The blast has the effects of 2nd-level [_scorching ray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=992) using a spell attack roll of +14, with its effect determined by the number of actions you used to

[Twilight Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1451)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant hooded lantern is made from onyx-black metal. In dim light, flecks of white metal speckled within the lantern's housing resemble stars in a night sky. The lantern uses oil as a standard hooded lantern, except that any light the lantern emits is converted into magical light similar to moonlight. This magical moonlight is always dim light.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You concentrate on the lantern's light and reduce or increase its radius. The light can have a minimum radius of 5 feet and a maximum radius equal to double the light's standard radius.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the lantern and unleash a beam of powerful moonlight, targeting a [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) effect within 60 feet. The lantern attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the effect with a counteract modifier of +10.

</column>

This elegant hooded lantern is made from onyx-black metal. In dim light, flecks of white metal speckled within the lantern's housing resemble stars in a night sky. The lantern uses oil as a standard hooded lantern, except that any light the lantern emits is converted into magical light similar to moonlight. This magical moonlight is always dim light.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;     **Effect** You concentrate on the lantern's light and reduce or increase its radius. The light can have a minimum radius of 5 feet and a maximum radius equal to double the light's standard radius.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the lantern and unleash a beam of powerful moonlight, targeting a [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) effect within 60 feet. The lantern attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the effect with a counteract modifier of +10.

May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands **Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 75 **Price** 13,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This staff is made from the femur of a [gold dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=173) wrapped in the multicolored scales of every type of [chromatic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=34) and [metallic dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=35). When wielding this staff, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52), [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50), [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51), or [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) to [dragons](/Traits.aspx?ID=50) and creatures with strong draconic ties (such as [kobolds](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=18), [dragon instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=2) [barbarians](/Classes.aspx?ID=2), and [draconic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=5) [sorcerers](/Classes.aspx?ID=11)). **Activate** command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Attempt a melee Strike with _Wyrm Drinker_. If your target is a dragon or a creature with a strong draconic tie, such as a kobold, and you damage the target, the target is [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), or drained 4 on a critical hit. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **2nd** [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=158) (dragons only)
  • **4th** [_reflective scales_](/Spells.aspx?ID=583)
  • **5th** [_summon dragon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=319)
  • **6th** [_dragon form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=88), _summon dragon_ (6th)
  • **7th** _summon dragon_ (7th)
name: Wyrm Drinker

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs=""
>
[Spellcutter](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1457)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 76

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The blade of this _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_ seems to absorb light from the air around it, giving the impression the wielder is standing in the shadow of a much larger creature. While _Spellcutter_ is a potent weapon for battling [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=35), an ancient restriction on the blade prevents it from harming living creatures; attacks against living beings deal no damage. This restriction doesn't apply to [dhampirs](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=23) or creatures with the [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42) ability who are otherwise living; the magic of the blade can't distinguish between such beings and undead.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You're targeted by a spell while wielding _Spellcutter_;  **Effect** _Spellcutter_ attempts to absorb the magic of the triggering spell, giving you a +4 status bonus to your AC and saves against the effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** Touching _Spellcutter_ to any light source of 12th level or lower (including 6th-level spells with the [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) trait) instantly extinguishes the effect. If the source of the light is a large flame, such as a forest fire or bonfire, it only extinguishes the light source in a 5-foot radius burst from the point of contact.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spellcutter

    right="Item 16"
    pfs=""
>
[Grimoire of Unknown Necessities](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1458)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 77

**Price** 8,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The cover of this grimoire is bound in a strange crimson, pebbly leather that a DC 40 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) identifies as the hide of a rare monster from the distant plane of Leng. The grimoire's spine is bound in copper and a spiral of shifting runes slowly swirls across its surface.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You're an [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) prepared spellcaster and you have rested but not yet prepared your spells for the day; **Effect** The grimoire provides you with temporary knowledge of a single spell of the highest level you're capable of casting that you don't already have recorded in it or any of your other spellbooks, chosen by the GM based on their knowledge of the adventure to be particularly useful to you during the upcoming day. A strange quirk of the tome prevents the spell's knowledge from ever traveling further than your own mind. The granted spell is not only removed from the tome's pages at the start of the next day, but is also removed from any other scroll, spellbook, or grimoire the granted spell is copied into, as well as any other way it might have been learned or copied.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grimoire of Unknown Necessities

[Pressure Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1460)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tightly sealed metal container is filled with highly pressurized gas that can explode to release a powerful shockwave. A pressure bomb deals the listed bludgeoning damage and splash damage. On a critical hit, the target is knocked prone. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls, and more powerful pressure bombs can knock creatures back.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Limited"
>
Pressure Bomb (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage and 1 bludgeoning splash damage. Tiny or smaller targets are pushed 5 feet away from you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Pressure Bomb (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 bludgeoning damage and 2 bludgeoning splash damage. Small or smaller targets are pushed 5 feet away from you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Limited"
>
Pressure Bomb (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 275 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 bludgeoning damage and 3 bludgeoning splash damage. Medium or smaller targets are pushed 5 feet away from you.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Limited"
>
Pressure Bomb (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d4 bludgeoning damage and 4 bludgeoning splash damage. Medium or smaller creatures who take splash damage are pushed 5 feet away from the target, and then, if the target is Large or smaller, it is pushed 5 feet away from you.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

---

It's possible for a desperate alchemist to rig the brain container of a defeated alchemical golem to create a volatile (if unusual) explosive. You throw the grenade up to 60 feet, and it explodes, dealing 3d6 fire damage and 3d6 mental damage in a 10-foot burst (DC 27 basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature takes 2d6 persistent fire damage.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a brain case harvested from [alchemical golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=239)

</column>

[Communication Pendants](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1463)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each of these matching necklaces has been constructed using one half of a purple teardrop-shaped gem, split in two. These pendants function only with the other pendant in their pair and must be crafted together. If one pendant becomes broken, the other shatters into non-magical shards. The Price listed above is for a pair of pendants. 

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You speak, audibly projecting your voice from the pendant you're wearing to the other. Your voice can be heard by whoever is wearing the other pendant no matter how far away the pendants are from each other, as long as the wearer has invested the other pendant and is on the same plane as you. Continued activation allows you to communicate with the other pendant's wearer whenever and for as long as your desire.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Communication Pendants

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

A _demolishing_ weapon is made to destroy constructs. Damage inflicted on a construct with a _demolishing_ weapon continues to spread throughout the creature—cracks form, linkages fail, surfaces erode—and otherwise dismantle its body. When you damage a construct using a _demolishing_ weapon, you deal an extra 1d6 persistent force damage. On a critical hit, you deal an extra 1d12 persistent force damage instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Demolishing

These intracately braided lenghts of leather, twince, and ribbon can be worn around the neck. _Empathic cords_ function only with the other cord in their pair and must be crafted together. If one cord becomes broken, the other dissolves in to non-magical dust. The Price listed above is for a pair of cords. The creatures wearing a pair of _empathic cords_ can sense each other's feelings and communicate empathically, no matter how far away they are, as long as the creatures are on the same planet. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), targeting the creature wearing the matching cord. The range of this message is planetary.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Librarian's Baton](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1466)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=43" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A crystal pendulum hangs loosely from a delicate cord connecting the tips of this Y-shaped wooden baton. Such batons are often used by librarians who have problems with customers (or thieves) who can't be trusted to return the books they borrow, but they're equally valuable to wizards who are particularly worried about having ther spellbooks stolen. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per month;   **Effect** You focus your attention on the crystal pendulum, repeatedly passing it over the covers of up to 10 Bulk of books. Doing so causes the _librarian's baton_ to permanently attune itself to those books, while simultaneously removing any attunement it may have had to previous books. If an attuned book is damage significantly, the _librarian's baton_ loses attunement to it (although it can be reattuned at a later date to the damaged book as long as it retains some of its functionality). The _librarian's baton_ grants a +2 item bonus to perception checks to Search for attuned books hidden within 60 feet of you. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** When you activate the _librarian's baton_, concentrate on one of the books to which the baton is attuned. The crystal pendulum moves of its own volition, pointing directly toward the location of the book you were concentrating on as a long as the book is within 25 miles of you. If the book is further away than this, or if the book has been damaged enough that it's no longer attuned, you learn only that the book is out of range or damaged, as appropriate. You can Sustain this activation up to 1 minute, during which time you can sense whether the target book is in motion, is in a creature's possession, or enters the possession of a different creature. However, you can't determine the identities of such creatures

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Librarian's Baton

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dirty metal wand is covered in an oily acrid smelling film. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140), but the grease is caustic. If you cast this spell on an area, any creature that fails their Reflex save and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), or that begins their turn prone, takes 1d6 acid damage. If you cast this spell on an unattended object, the first creature to pick up the object during the duration takes 1d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). If you cast this spell on an attended object and the holder or wielder fails their [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) or Reflex save, the holder or wielder takes 1d6 persistent acid damage. If you cast this spell on a worn object, any creature who [Grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) or [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the wearer takes 1d6 acid damage.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wand of Caustic Effluence

These wire spectacles have been fitted with circular glass lenses. While wearing the spectacles, you gain a +1 item bonus to Decipher Writing.

You can understand the meaning of the language you are reading. This doesn't let you understand codes, riddles, or metaphors (subject to the GM's discretion). This effect lasts 1 hour.

[Boom Snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1469)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Mechanical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=105" />

---

By combining a [lesser alchemist's fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75), a [ lesser thunderstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=79), and other catalysts with a pressure sensitive trigger, you create a snare that creates a fiery and thunderous explosion when triggered. The target and all creatures in adjacent squares take 2d6 fire damage and 2d6 sonic damage with a basic DC 19 Reflex save. On a failure, they are deafened for 1d4 rounds and take 1d4 persistent fire damage.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a [lesser alchemist's fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75) and a [ lesser thunderstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=79).

</column>

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105) **Price** 25 gp --- Complex traps that incorporate ooze monsters into their ingenious construction have long been a favorite of those who seek devious ways to protect their hidden laboratories, and it is from this tradition that the clinging ooze snare takes its inspiration. With its ingenious mixture of rare reagents, dried protoplasm harvested from ooze creatures, and a little pinch of alchemy, this snare triggers into an ooze-like hazard that temporarily mimics the dangers presented by a creature like a sewer ooze or other carnivorous protoplasmic monster. When a creature triggers a clinging ooze snare, the snare creates a short lived protoplasmic mass that lashes out in all directions, dealing 2d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 acid damage. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 21 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected
**Success** The creature takes half damage
**Failure** The target takes full damage and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of their next turn as the ooze impedes their movement.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and is both [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) and [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round. The target can [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 21) to end this effect early.
name: Clinging Ooze Snare

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spear of the Destroyer's Flame](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1472)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 16

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=6)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The tip of this _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greater flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=304) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=6)_ is edged with several razor-sharp obsidian blades. The spear has the [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) weapon trait, in addition to the normal weapon traits for a longspear.

 While the _Spear of the Destroyer's Flame_ is in your possession, you gain [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) resistance 20. When you critically hit a creature with the spear, the creature's blood begins to boil, unleashing blasts of heat from the wound. The creature takes 2d8 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), in addition to the persistent fire damage from the _greater flaming_ rune; all creatures adjacent to the creature when it takes the persistent fire damage take an equal amount of fire damage. Like the fire damage from the greater flaming rune, this fire damage ignores a creature's fire resistance, though it doesn't ignore fire immunity.

 In addition to its deadliness as a weapon, the _Spear of the Destroyer's Flame_ gives its wielder full control over the Army of Fire, though the orders of the Commander of Flame supersede the orders of anyone wielding the spear, as noted below. While the spear is in your possession, you're aware of the location of the Army of Fire and the army's distance from you. In addition, you can give orders to the Army of Fire, and the army's members will understand and obey you, regardless of what language you speak. If you're within 1 mile of the Army of Fire, the army and its members gain a +10 circumstance bonus to their Speeds. Any members of the Army of Fire within 100 feet of you become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) and can use the additional action to Step, Stride, or Strike. The army remains quickened for 1 minute after moving further than 100 feet from you.

 The Army of Fire has unwavering loyalty to the Commander of Flame, an individual who has completed a special activation that ties their soul to the spear. As long as the Army of Fire can see and hear the Commander of Flame, the army will follow the Commander's orders. The Commander of Flame can sense the presence of the Army of Fire and issue orders, regardless of language, with the same effects as for someone in possession of the spear. The Commander of Flame still needs the spear in hand to muster the Army of Fire, however.

 If the Commander of Flame doesn't hold the _Spear of the Destroyer's Flame_ for 1 week, it returns to [Cuetzmonquali's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1722) gut, instantly teleporting to the creature's location. If Cuetzmonquali is dead at this time, the spear also resurrects the creature, using the effects of a critical success on a 10th-level [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) ritual.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You attempt to tie your soul to the spear. If the current Commander of Flame still lives, you must attempt either a Will save, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check, or [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check. The DC of this check is equal to the current Commander's Will DC, Diplomacy DC, Intimidation DC, or Warfare Lore DC, whichever is highest. On a success, you become the Commander of Flame. On a failure, the current Commander knows your name and appearance and is aware of your exact location for 24 hours.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 hour (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You use the spear's power to muster the Army of Fire. You can choose to either call the Army of Fire to your location or move yourself to the army's location. When calling the army, you select an area you can see within 500 feet. The army is instantly teleported to the location you select, spreading out as necessary to arrive safely. When moving yourself, you instantly teleport yourself to a location within 500 feet of the army. The army is composed of souls given form as burning humanoids, elementals, spirits, and undead.

**Destruction** If the entire Army of Fire is destroyed and the Commander of Flame is slain with the _Spear of the Destroyer's Flame_, the spear shatters into a massive explosion of flame, dealing 20d10 fire damage in a 30-foot burst (DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). In addition, the Commander's soul burns away to nothing, destroying the Commander forever and releasing the bound souls of the Army of Fire to the River of Souls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spear of the Destroyer's Flame

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ridill](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1473)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 29

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This 12-foot sword was made by a [cyclops](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=25) in the ancient past. It might be the greatest dragon-killing weapon ever made, and [Fafnheir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1725) justly fears it, which is why he keeps it hidden in his hoard. Runes of dragon slaying are written down its blade. _Ridill_ is a _+3 [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [dragon bane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1041) [speed](/Equipment.aspx?ID=304) [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24)_. While wielding _Ridill_, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110). This increases to +4 if the effect is from a [dragon](/Traits.aspx?ID=50). If you have an ability that depends on Large weapons (such as [barbarian giant instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=4)), it works with _Ridill_.

 _Ridill_ is always considered to have the appropriate material or damage type necessary to deactivate a dragon's [regeneration](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=26) or cause the dragon's weakness to apply. For example, _Ridill_ is considered to be [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) when attacking a [linnorm](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=69), while it would be treated as having dealt cold damage when attacking a [red dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=168). If the dragon has more than one weakness, only the highest value applies.

**Destruction** If you boil _Ridill_ in the blood of 10 different types of dragon ([blue dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=166) dragon, red dragon, etc.) and drop the eye of a cyclops seer into the brew, _Ridill_ melts away into nothingness.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ridill

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 35 --- Highly desired for the prestige, the pelt of the grogrisant also has invaluable properties. Standard-grade items can be made from the pelt of a [grisantian lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1728), but high-grade items require the pelt of the [grogrisant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1727) itself. This supernaturally tough animal skin is gold and shines in sunlight. It can be used to make any item usually made of cloth, leather, or hide. This material is immune to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), and its Hardness is doubled against piercing or slashing damage. Up to 4 Bulk of grisantian pelt can be harvested from a single creature (this is usually more than enough for 4 suits of standard grade armor or 2 suits of high-grade armor). ## Grisantian Pelt Items

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mantle of the Grogrisant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1476)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 35

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, golden cloak is among the royal regalia of the emperors of Taldor. It was fashioned from the pelt of [Grogrisant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1727) itself, after being slain by Taldaris, and Grogrisant's mane was fashioned into a thick fringe that runs all along the mantle's edges. The paws of the Grogrisant cross beneath the wearer's throat, giving you an imposing appearance. The _Mantle of the Grogrisant_ grants you [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) resistance 15 and physical resistance 5 (except bludgeoning).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision, Interact ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pull the hood of the mantle over your face, revealing the six eyes of Grogrisant. The mantle gives off a flash of blinding light in a 60-foot emanation. All enemies within this area must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude saving throw. On a failure, they're [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for 1 minute. On a critical failure, they're permanently blinded. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mantle of the Grogrisant

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Devil's Trident](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1477)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 47

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45)_ is made of an ancient black metal that glistens unnaturally and is cold to the touch. The prongs of this trident drip with metallic lake water. Strikes with the _Devil's Trident_ trigger the weaknesses of any creature with a weakness to water.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Command;   **Trigger** You critically hit a creature;  **Effect** The target must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become infected with [sewer haze](/Diseases.aspx?ID=11).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The trident casts 3rd-level [_quench_](/Spells.aspx?ID=709) with a DC of 25 and a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier of +15. You can Sustain the Spell as normal.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call upon the corrupted power of the trident to stay a foe. Attempt a Strike. On a hit, the trident casts 3rd-level [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) on the target (DC 25). If the Strike was a critical hit, the target is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) even if it critically succeeds at the save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Devil's Trident

markdown:
**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 59 **Usage** worn --- This coin-sized golden pin depicts a bundle of five trimmed tree branches. Once you've stolen the pin, accepted it from [Krampus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1213) willingly, or carried it for at least 1 minute, its curse activates. After that happens, the pin fastens itself to your clothing, providing you an insistent empathic admonition not to be naughty and constantly informing Krampus of your location, as [_status_](/Spells.aspx?ID=308). The pin reappears and reattaches itself within moments if discarded or destroyed. The pin constantly monitors your actions, judging you against a [good-aligned champion's code of conduct](/Tenets.aspx?ID=1), plus the following third tenet:
  • You must never knowingly steal or inflict harm with the goal of stealing the victim's wealth. This tenet doesn't prevent you from looting those you harm primarily in the defense of yourself and others.
Whenever you violate this code of conduct, one of the golden branches tarnishes (and utterly egregious violations might tarnish multiple branches). During the winter following the curse activating, the pin transforms based on the number of golden branches remaining. **5 Branches**: The pin either transforms into a pile of golden trinkets whose gold piece value equals your level × 25, or it grants you absolution for your misdeeds as a critically successful [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2) ritual for any good-aligned faith. You don't need to perform a special quest; your exemplary behavior over the past year qualifies. **3–4 Branches**: The pin either becomes a non-magical golden pin worth 10 gp, or it grants you absolution for your misdeeds as a successful _atone_ ritual for any good-aligned faith. **1–2 Branches**: The pin transforms into a leering Krampus face. As long as you wear it, it grants you weakness 5 to bludgeoning damage, and you still can't remove it. **0 Branches**: The pin transforms into Krampus, who appears in an adjacent space and knows of your misdeeds while wearing the pin.
name: Golden Rod Memento

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gamepiece Chariot](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1479)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 65

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This stone figurine of a chariot pulled by two horses was used by Tekritanin adults during strategy game competitions. Sometimes, though, mischievous adolescents snatched pieces like this one for joyriding.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You place the figurine on the ground and roll it forward. As the chariot begins to move, it rapidly expands to the size of a Large [heavy chariot](/Vehicles.aspx?ID=4) pulled by two animated stone [war horses](/Monsters.aspx?ID=268). You can board the chariot and drive it up to 80 feet in a straight line. You can move through spaces occupied by creatures of size Medium or smaller, dealing 4d8+10 bludgeoning damage to those creatures. Affected creatures must attempt a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save; on a failure, a creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The chariot returns to its figurine form once it stops moving.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Sandstorm Top](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1480)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

On Tekritanin holidays, children reenacted legendary battles of deities by playing with magical tops. Though the paint on this wooden top has long since faded, its magical runes once gleamed yellow-gold and fiery orange, evoking the color of the sky during a sandstorm. To set the top in motion, wind a string around its bottom and upward along its body, and then throw it while gripping the string.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Ineteract;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You throw the _sandstorm top_ 10 feet away from you. For 1 minute, as it spins, the top generates a magical sandstorm in a 5-foot radius. Any creature that starts its turn in the area or moves into the area takes 4d6 slashing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Additionally, a creature within the sandstorm must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sandstorm Top

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

---

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ is made from wood, horn, and sinew. Once wielded by Ninshaburian heretics, it's carried in a leather case etched with images of Namzaruum in combat with [Somnalu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1737). On a successful Strike, it deals an additional 1d6 bludgeoning damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You make a ranged Strike. On a hit, a vortex of air trails the arrow as it travels. When the arrow strikes its target, the vortex expands to a 30-foot radius from its point of impact. The arrow deals an additional 1d8 bludgeoning damage against its target. For the next 3 minutes, any physical ranged attacks that would strike a target within the vortex deal an additional 1d8 bludgeoning damage. The vortex is difficult terrain, and creatures attempting to fly through the vortex using a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action must attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save, losing its move action on a failed save. Any gases within the vortex are cleared by the winds.

</column>

>
[Star Chart Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1484)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 107

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo consists of numerous asterisks at regular intervals along the fingers of one hand, perfect for measuring stars against the horizon. When used against a clear night sky, the tattoo grants a +2 circumstance bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=64) and counts as a [compass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=16) for the purpose of that action.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The dots of the tattoo turn to points of light and fly above the head of a creature within 30 feet, where they form a three-star constellation that guides your spells further than they could normally travel. The constellation sheds dim light and remains visible even if the creature hides, aiding in locating it. Furthermore, while the constellation lasts, if you Cast a Spell that has a range and that spell would affect only the marked creature, the spell's range is increased by 30 feet. As is standard for increasing spell ranges, if the spell normally has a range of touch, you extend its range to 30 feet. Each time you use this benefit, one of the stars above the creature's head winks out; when all three have winked out or after 1 minute has passed, the effect ends.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Star Chart Tattoo

This _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [combat grapnel](/Weapons.aspx?ID=139)_ takes the form of a fishhook made of scavenged bones and stone with a cord of braided water. Attacks with a _tidal fishhook_ don't take any of the normal penalties for fighting underwater. A [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) (but not destroyed) tidal fishhook can be fully repaired by submerging it in an ocean for 1 minute.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** A _tidal fishhook_ can travel between bodies of water to strike distant foes. You throw the fishhook into a 5-foot square that's fully covered with water, and it emerges to Strike or [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a target from another such square within 60 feet; the squares can be part of the same body of water or different ones. The distance between the bodies of water doesn't count toward the fishhook's range increment. If you successfully Grapple the target as part of this Strike, you can pull yourself along the fishhook's line, teleporting to the secondary body of water.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tidal Fishhook

You construct this snare around a sealed bladder containing substances that local predators find delicious, such as scent glands from prey animals or fresh carrion. For 1 hour, animals attracted to the flavor (such as most predators, at the GM's discretion) can smell the creature from double the usual distance of their scent and are likely to approach to investigate the smell. A creature can wash away the appetizing flavor with 1 minute of vigorous scrubbing.

When a creature enters the square, the vessel's neck shatters, dispersing the powders into the air. The creature triggering the snare must attempt a DC 20 Reflex save to avoid getting powder in its face. A creature [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) or [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) by the powder can use an Interact action to attempt a DC 10 flat check; on a success, it removes the condition. On a failure, it can try again, with a DC of 5 on the next Interact check. If it fails a second time, it automatically removes the condition with a third Interact action. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is dazzled for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is blinded until the end of its next turn and dazzled for 1 minute afterwards.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 minute and dazzled for 10 minutes afterwards.

This snare forces air through several high-pitched, whistle-like pipes or reeds. The shrill noise can be heard clearly up to 2 miles away but is most intense near the snare's trigger. The snare deals 4d6 sonic damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) to creatures within 10 feet; a creature that fails this save is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 round (1 hour on a critical failure). The triggering creature is deafened for 1 minute on a failure, or 1 hour on a critical failure.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Piercing Whistle Snare

markdown:
**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 113 **Price** 75 gp --- This snare emits puffs of oxidizing chemicals that rapidly degrade metal items; skymetals and most precious metals are immune. The chemicals affect a non-magical metal item of up to 1 Bulk that the triggering creature is holding or openly wearing (determined randomly if the creature is holding or wearing more than one). The triggering creature must attempt a DC 25 Reflex saving throw. The snare deals 2d6 damage to the item; this damage ignores the item's Hardness and might deal persistent damage on a failed Reflex save. The creature, or an adjacent creature, can attempt to scrape off the chemicals as an Interact action to prevent the persistent damage; doing so reduces the DC of the flat check to end persistent damage to 10 and grants an immediate flat check. Thin iron or steel items, such as weapons, typically have 20 HP and a Broken Threshold of 10, and thicker iron or steel items, such as most suits of armor, typically have 36 HP and a Broken Threshold of 18. Other materials' statistics can be found on [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=730) and the statistics for items made of [precious materials](/Equipment.aspx?Category=22) on their respective pages. **Critical Success** The object is unaffected.
**Success** The object takes full damage.
**Failure** The object takes full damage as well as 1d6 [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Critical Failure** The object takes double damage as well as 2d6 persistent damage.
name: Rusting Snare

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ivory Baton](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1492)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monsters of Myth](/Sources.aspx?ID=107) pg. 125

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This white, slender rod bears intricately carved reliefs of magnificent animals that bow and dance to some unseen commander. The baton enables you to command [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=35).

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You take control of a construct. This has the effects of [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) with a DC of 30, but it affects only constructs. It lacks the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) trait and functions on [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) constructs. It also ignores any [golem antimagic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=57) the target might have. The effect's duration ends if the baton is destroyed or leaves your possession for at least 1 minute, and you can issue commands to the target only while holding the baton.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You closely direct the actions of one construct you control and can see within 60 feet. That construct gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saving throws (except saving throws against the _ivory baton_).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Qat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1493)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Drug" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=261" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 391

**Price** 4 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While initially used by Qadiran soldiers and warriors going to battle, this stimulant and euphoriant soon became popular among civilian Qadirans from diverse social strata. Some of the latter close up shop for three to four hours after the midday meal to socialize and chew qat leaves. User experiences with qat vary from feeling energetic, euphoric, talkative, and alert to experiencing depression and hallucination, depending on the quality and type of qat leaves.

The save for addiction to qat is DC 14, but the maximum addiction stage of qat never progresses beyond stage 1. 

**Saving Throw** DC 14 Fortitude; **Onset** 10 minutes; **Maximum Duration** 8 hours; **Stage 1** +1 item bonus to Reflex saves (10 minutes); **Stage 2** [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 hour)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Grit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1494)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Drug" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=261" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 391

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

First developed in the Puddles about 20 years ago, this stimulant has grown from a diversion of the wretched poor to a habit for well- heeled merchants and nobles. At low doses, grit provides the physical body with increased energy while stimulating the idle mind into effortless flights of creative fancy. Abuse of the drug leads to severe hallucinations, numbing, and uncontrollable rage. Grit is produced by grinding down alchemically-enhanced barbarian chew, which is mixed with toxic plant- based substances, such as bitterbark or redroot. The powder is very expensive and highly addictive.

The save for addiction to grit is DC 15, and the addiction has the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) trait.

 **Saving Throw** DC 15 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 4 hours; **Stage 1** +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks (10 minutes); **Stage 2** +1 item bonus to Athletics, Crafting, and Performance checks, –2 item penalty to Perception checks (1 hour); **Stage 3** –4 item penalty to Perception checks (1 hour); **Stage 4** as stage 3 (1 hour); **Stage 5** [confusion](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) (1 round); **Stage 6** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) (remaining duration)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grit

This highly addictive drug is produced from the crushed bones of [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28). Demon dust strengthens the inhaler's body for acts requiring great physical stamina. While the drug is in effect, users typically experience some form of memory loss. Others claim to have experienced hallucinations, flashbacks, and confusion for a few hours after the drug wears off.

The save for addiction to demon dust is DC 24, and the addiction has the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) trait.

 **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 8 hours; **Stage 1** +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks and treat up to 4 Bulk as being negligible in weight (1 minute); **Stage 2** +2 item bonus to Athletics checks, treat up to 4 Bulk as being negligible in weight, and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 hour); **Stage 3** stupefied 1 (1 hour); **Stage 4** stupefied 2 (1 hour); **Stage 5** stupefied 2 and all memories made since you were first exposed to this dose of demon dust become hazy and vague (1 hour); **Stage 6** stupefied 2 and all memories made in the previous 24 hours become hazy and vague (1 hour)

>
[Succubus Kiss](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1496)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Drug" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=261" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Absalom, City of Lost Omens](/Sources.aspx?ID=108) pg. 391

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rare and highly addictive drug is rumored to be created by wizards working alongside [succubi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=98). Taken in the form of lozenges, this drug can heighten the body's pleasure and pain stimuli as well as increase alertness, energy, and euphoria. Serious side effects that can occur include heart attack, stroke, and sudden vision and hearing loss, but these risks do little to deter users.

The save for [addiction](/Diseases.aspx?ID=15) to succubus kiss is DC 28, and the addiction has the [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=162) trait.

 **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 8 hours; **Stage 1** +2 item bonus to initiative checks (10 minutes) and to all saving throws against [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects; **Stage 2** as stage 1 plus [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (1 hour); **Stage 3** as stage 2 plus [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) or [deafness](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) (1 hour); **Stage 4** blindness and deafness (1 hour); **Stage 5** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Succubus Kiss

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bubbling Scale](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1497)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Threshold of Knowledge</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Threshold of Knowledge](/Sources.aspx?ID=109) pg. 10

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This dark, iridescent scale is about the size of a small coin. When you swallow the scale, you immediately grow a patch of scales that cover the majority of your body. For 1 hour, you can hold your breath for 15 rounds plus your Constitution modifier (instead of 5 rounds + your Constitution modifier) before [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). It has no effect in non-aquatic environments that require you to hold your breath. After this time, the scales wither and fall off your body.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bubbling Scale

[Lion Badge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1498)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Threshold of Knowledge</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Threshold of Knowledge](/Sources.aspx?ID=109) pg. 10

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition as a result of a Will save

**Requirement** You're an expert in Will saves.
</row>

</column>

---

This plain wooden badge has the face of a roaring lion etched onto its surface. When you activate this talisman, reduce the value of your frightened condition by 1 (to a minimum of 0). When this talisman is used, the image of the lion fades and the item becomes a plain, non-magical wooden badge.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Seeking Bracelets](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1499)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Threshold of Knowledge</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Threshold of Knowledge](/Sources.aspx?ID=109) pg. 10

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn bracelet

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>envision, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Both beaded bracelets in this pair have an even larger bead in their center. The bracelets are magically linked and only interact with their counterpart from the same pair. When you crush the largest bead to activate the bracelet, it creates a mental alert in the mind of the wearer of the paired bracelet. This alert works at any distance as long as both bracelets are on the same plane.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Seeking Bracelets

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Threshold of Knowledge</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Threshold of Knowledge](/Sources.aspx?ID=109) pg. 10

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The blade of this _+1 [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9)_ is engraved to look like the curving feather of a particularly elaborate quill. The wooden handle has a pen nib at the base. You can use the _wordreaper's_ handle as an ink pen and it never runs out of ink.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You use the _wordreaper_ to inscribe the words “read by” and your name inside a non-magical literary work (such as a non-magical book, scroll, or play). You immediately gain cursory knowledge of the literary work as though you had skimmed it for 5 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wordreaper

    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Tallow Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1501)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A mixture of rendered animal fat and acids designed to ignite the fat when exposed to air, a tallow bomb creates a splash of burning oil that adheres to skin, clothes, and hair. A tallow bomb deals the listed fire damage, [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and splash damage. On a critical hit, a living creature taking persistent fire damage from a tallow bomb is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) from the stench of burning fat and can't reduce its sickened value below 1 while the persistent fire damage lasts. Many types of tallow bombs grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Tallow Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1501)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A mixture of rendered animal fat and acids designed to ignite the fat when exposed to air, a tallow bomb creates a splash of burning oil that adheres to skin, clothes, and hair. A tallow bomb deals the listed fire damage, [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and splash damage. On a critical hit, a living creature taking persistent fire damage from a tallow bomb is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) from the stench of burning fat and can't reduce its sickened value below 1 while the persistent fire damage lasts. Many types of tallow bombs grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

[Twigjack Sack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1502)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sharp, flexible brambles poke from this sack made of intricately intertwined plant fibers. The sack's contents creak under the strain of the tightly compressed bundle. 

When thrown, a twigjack sack bursts open, spraying brambles in all directions that gouge and slash nearby creatures. A twigjack sack deals the listed piercing damage, [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and splash damage. Many types of twigjack sacks grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

</column>

Sharp, flexible brambles poke from this sack made of intricately intertwined plant fibers. The sack's contents creak under the strain of the tightly compressed bundle. 

When thrown, a twigjack sack bursts open, spraying brambles in all directions that gouge and slash nearby creatures. A twigjack sack deals the listed piercing damage, [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and splash damage. Many types of twigjack sacks grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

---

The crown of this stitched leather cap is sewn in the shape of a small bowl. Fixed inside the bowl is a melted nub of wax with a small black wick.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You shake your head, and the candle wick ignites. The _candlecap_ sheds dim light in a 20-foot radius. The candle doesn't require oxygen and can't be smothered or quenched. Activating the _candlecap_ again douses the light.

    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ember Dust](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1504)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 76

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This handful of gritty black dust glows faintly, like old coals, but is cool to the touch. When _ember dust_ is sprinkled upon the ground or a flat surface in an adjacent unoccupied square, the dust transforms into a bonfire that fills the majority of the square. For 8 hours, the bonfire blazes regardless of whether it has any fuel. The fire doesn't harm the surface on which it was sprinkled, and it can't be extinguished except by fully dousing or submerging it in water, or by smothering it completely. Anything that's lit from the bonfire requires fuel to burn and can be quenched normally. While it's safe enough to move through the space and edge around the bonfire, a creature remaining in the bonfire for at least a full round takes 1d6 fire damage each round. After 8 hours, the bonfire becomes a normal fire and continues burning only as conditions permit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ember Dust

>
[Mad Mammoth's Juke](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1505)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 76

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You would be injured by an effect that would bury you, such as an avalanche or a tunnel collapse

**Requirement** You are an expert in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1).
</row>

</column>

---

This small, fragile tusk came from a very young mammoth. It's filled with ice-cold glacial water and capped with a carved piece of ivory. When you crush the tusk, the water rushes around you in an instant as you twist away from impending disaster with a supernatural flourish. You teleport to an unoccupied space within 200 feet that you can see, bringing along all your equipment. You can't bring any other creature with you, even in an [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) space.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mad Mammoth's Juke

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 76

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This golden flower grows amid filthy ponds or puddles, its spotless yellow petals sharply contrasting its soiled surroundings. You can pin a _mudlily_ to your clothing or your hair to enjoy some of its magical sullying and cleaning properties. While you wear a clean _mudlily_, you gradually become dirty over the course of an hour; grime and mud subtly manifest from nowhere onto your clothes, hair, skin, and all of your possessions other than the _mudlily_. After just 1 hour, you appear as though you've been living in squalor for years. This filth can be washed away normally, but it inevitably returns as long as you continue to wear a clean _mudlily_.

 If you soil the _mudlily_ with some dirt or mud, as long as you wear the flower, you instead become clean over an hour. Any gunk soiling your person vanishes completely from everywhere on your body except the _mudlily_. Each day at dawn, the _mudlily_ instantly becomes clean again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mudlily
