    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Broken Tusk Pendant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1508)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The followers of the Broken Tusk have passed down their custom of crafting and gifting these small ivory necklaces for generations. They're typically made from mammoth tusk (though any type of ivory will do), and each is carved to resemble a particular kind of animal—usually a raven, a moose, an ox, or an otter. Because tradition dictates that Broken Tusk followers take ivory only from already-dead animals, _Broken Tusk pendants_ are especially rare; if an individual loses their pendant, it might be years before they acquire the materials necessary to fashion a replacement. _Broken Tusk pendants_ bear no magical powers if they are made for oneself; only gifted pendants have true magical properties.

 You wear a _Broken Tusk pendant_ like jewelry or tie it to your clothing. A _Broken Tusk pendant_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks; this bonus increases to +2 when you [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) of the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) targets you with a melee attack, and you can see the animal;  **Effect** You gain a +1 item bonus to AC against the triggering attack, or a +2 item bonus if the animal is the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Broken Tusk Pendant
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 50 gp

</row>

level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Limited"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 77

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The pendant grants a +2 item bonus to Nature checks (+3 to Command an Animal of the type depicted by the pendant). Once per day, when you use the _Broken Tusk pendant's_ activation, if the animal misses you, the _Broken Tusk pendant_ casts [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=87) on the animal (DC 30). You can choose whether to use this once-per-day effect after the animal misses you.
name: Broken Tusk Pendant

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Broken Tusk Pendant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1508)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The followers of the Broken Tusk have passed down their custom of crafting and gifting these small ivory necklaces for generations. They're typically made from mammoth tusk (though any type of ivory will do), and each is carved to resemble a particular kind of animal—usually a raven, a moose, an ox, or an otter. Because tradition dictates that Broken Tusk followers take ivory only from already-dead animals, _Broken Tusk pendants_ are especially rare; if an individual loses their pendant, it might be years before they acquire the materials necessary to fashion a replacement. _Broken Tusk pendants_ bear no magical powers if they are made for oneself; only gifted pendants have true magical properties.

 You wear a _Broken Tusk pendant_ like jewelry or tie it to your clothing. A _Broken Tusk pendant_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) checks; this bonus increases to +2 when you [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) of the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) targets you with a melee attack, and you can see the animal;  **Effect** You gain a +1 item bonus to AC against the triggering attack, or a +2 item bonus if the animal is the specific kind depicted by the pendant.

This snare consists of a flat rock or metal plate held in place with a spring or flexible branch. When triggered, the flat object swings wide and smacks the triggering creature violently. The first creature to enter its square receives 2d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature is also [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).

May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame **Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 79 **Price** 12 gp --- This simple trap is made of taut twigs that, when triggered, snap down on a pollen-filled sac of a peculiar flower. When inhaled, the pollen imparts a hazy state of profound agreeability. The first creature that enters the snare's space triggers the snare. The creature that triggered the trap must attempt a DC 18 Will save. [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures and creatures that don't breathe are immune to dream pollen. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 1 for 1 round. For 1 minute, the creature becomes [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18) to all creatures to which it was [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25), or [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21) to all creatures to which it was friendly. If the creature was [unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=40) or [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) to any creatures, it remains unfriendly or hostile to them. If the creature is an [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), any creature to which it's friendly or helpful can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on it and to make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of it.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration of the agreeability is 1 hour instead of 1 minute.
name: Dream Pollen Snare

May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame **Source** [Pathfinder #175: Broken Tusk Moon](/Sources.aspx?ID=112) pg. 79 **Price** 10 gp --- This snare consists of several narrow, sharpened stakes, each over a foot long, clustered together and pointing upward. The snare is patently obvious unless planted amid tall reeds or grasses or in someplace dark. Medium and smaller creatures can pass among the stakes easily; unlike most snares, the hoof stakes snare triggers only when a Large or larger creature enters its square. The snare deals 2d6 piercing damage to the triggering creature, which must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage, and it takes a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed for 1 minute, or until the creature or an ally spends three Interact actions to carefully remove the stakes.
**Failure** The triggering creature takes full damage. The creature takes a –5-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed, and any time it deals damage with a foot Strike (including as part of [Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39) or a similar ability), the creature takes 2d6 piercing damage. The effect lasts for 1 minute, or until the creature or an ally spends three Interact actions to carefully remove the stakes.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the circumstance penalty to its Speed is –10 feet, and the creature takes 4d6 damage whenever it deals damage with a foot Strike.
name: Hoof Stakes Snare

You set several barbed animal spines or wooden stakes in the ground, each tipped with poison from a venomous animal or toxic plant. The snare deals 1d4 piercing damage to the first creature to enter its square (DC 15 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a failed save, several barbs lodge in the creature's feet. For 1 minute (or until a creature or an ally spends three Interact actions to remove the barbs), the barbs deal 1d4 poison damage to the creature each time it Strides more than half its Speed.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 23"
    pfs=""
>
[Primordial Flame](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1513)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 75

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Created by the Kellid goddess [Sister Cinder](/Deities.aspx?ID=16) to lead her believers out of the Age of Darkness, this hand-held lamp is constructed from animal bone, hide, and sinew, and it contains an eternal flame that requires neither fuel nor oxygen to burn. The lamp's flame can be covered or hidden, but it can't be smothered or quenched. While uncovered, the _Primordial Flame_ sheds bright light in a 100-foot radius (and dim light for the next 100 feet). If the light passes through an area of magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) or targets a creature affected by magical darkness, the _Primordial Flame_ attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the darkness with a counteract level of 10 and a counteract modifier of +35.

 The presence of the _Primordial Flame_ alters the surrounding environment, changing weather patterns, temperature, climate, and even seasons. Within a 10-mile radius of the _Primordial Flame_, the temperature remains 70° F (21° C) during the day and 60° F (16° C) at night. The weather is calm and free from extreme weather conditions (such as blizzards, hail, thunderstorms, and tornadoes), and the sky remains clear. Plants in the area flourish as if they were receiving optimal sunlight, water, and nutrients for their species, despite the lack of precipitation. If the _Primordial Flame_ remains in the same region for an extended time, the area of this effect increases (typically by 1 mile each year, though terrain and other factors might alter this limit). During the time Venexus carried the _Primordial Flame_, its environmental reach expanded to cover the entirety of Lost Mammoth Valley.

 **Cursed** The _Primordial Flame_ was cursed by Syarstik Painted-Tiger, the guardian spirit of Red Cat Cave. As long as it remains cursed, a creature that holds the _Primordial Flame_ can't willingly give up possession. A creature that has held the _Primordial Flame_ and no longer has it in their possession is wracked with mental anguish, becoming [clumsy 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [stupefied 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15); these conditions can't be reduced or removed until the creature regains possession of the _Primordial Flame_ or the curse is removed. The curse can be removed only by avenging Syarstik.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** The _Primordial Flame's_ light intensifies, shedding bright sunlight in a 10-mile radius (and dim light to the next mile) for 1 hour. If the light passes through an area of magical darkness or targets a creature affected by magical darkness, the _Primordial Flame_ attempts to counteract the darkness with a counteract level of 10 and a counteract modifier of +35.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _Primordial Flame_ casts a 7th-level [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=326) spell. If you can cast spells higher than 7th level, the _Primordial Flame_ automatically heightens _sunburst_ to half your level rounded up.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;     **Effect** The _Primordial Flame_ casts a 5th-level [_produce flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) spell. If you can cast spells higher than 5th level, the _Primordial Flame_ automatically heightens _produce flame_ to half your level rounded up.

**Destruction** If used to slay a herald of Sister Cinder while on the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11), the _Primordial Flame_ is destroyed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Primordial Flame

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This flask contains fast-growing mold spores, which quickly take root but just as quickly rot away. A pernicious spore bomb deals the listed poison damage, [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Limited"
>
Pernicious Spore Bomb (Lesser)
</title>

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 78

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1 poison damage, 1d4 persistent piercing damage, and 1 poison splash damage. Except on a critical failure, one square of the target creature's space is coated in vegetation, becoming difficult terrain for 1 round.

right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>

</title>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Limited"

</title>

</traits>

---

level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Limited"
>
Pernicious Spore Bomb (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Quickmelt Slick](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1516)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This clay jar is filled with a warm fluid composed primarily of [lagofir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1777) oil and natural fire retardants. You can pour the fluid in an empty area adjacent to you, or over an adjacent frozen object, with an Interact action. The fluid instantly melts non-magical ice and snow in the area and harmlessly vaporizes the resulting meltwater. When used to melt magical ice and snow, quickmelt slick attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with the listed counteract modifier to melt the ice and snow, using the source of that ice and snow to determine the counteract level and DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Quickmelt Slick (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 78

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Lesser quickmelt slick melts ice and snow up to 1 foot in depth in one 5-foot square, or melts the ice covering a Small or smaller object. The slick has a +6 modifier for the counteract check and a counteract level of 1.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"
>
Quickmelt Slick (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 78

**Price** 13 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Moderate quickmelt slick melts ice and snow up to 2 feet in depth in two adjacent 5-foot squares, or melts the ice covering a Medium or smaller object. The slick has a +8 modifier for the counteract check and a counteract level of 2.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Limited"
>
Quickmelt Slick (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 78

**Price** 310 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Greater quickmelt slick melts ice and snow up to 5 feet in depth in a 10-foot-square area or melts the ice covering a Large or smaller object. The slick has a +19 modifier for the counteract check and a counteract level of 6.
name: Quickmelt Slick

[Quickmelt Slick](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1516)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This clay jar is filled with a warm fluid composed primarily of [lagofir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1777) oil and natural fire retardants. You can pour the fluid in an empty area adjacent to you, or over an adjacent frozen object, with an Interact action. The fluid instantly melts non-magical ice and snow in the area and harmlessly vaporizes the resulting meltwater. When used to melt magical ice and snow, quickmelt slick attempts a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with the listed counteract modifier to melt the ice and snow, using the source of that ice and snow to determine the counteract level and DC.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Water Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1517)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Nonlethal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=188" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bladder of water explodes when put under pressure or upon being punctured, dealing minimal damage, neutralizing acids, and dousing flames. A water bomb deals the listed bludgeoning damage and bludgeoning [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. On a hit, if the target is suffering from [persistent acid or persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), it can attempt a flat check to end that persistent damage immediately. As this is particularly effective assistance, the DC of the flat check is reduced from 15 to 10 for this check. On a hit against an unattended, non-magical fire, the bomb extinguishes the fire, or extinguishes one square of fire for a larger fire. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls, and some types extinguish wider areas of fire.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Limited"
>
Water Bomb (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 79

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1 nonlethal bludgeoning damage and 1 nonlethal bludgeoning splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Water Bomb (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 79

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 1d4 nonlethal bludgeoning damage and 2 nonlethal bludgeoning splash damage.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Limited"
>
Water Bomb (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 79

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 nonlethal bludgeoning damage and 3 nonlethal bludgeoning splash damage. Except on a critical failure, the bomb extinguishes non-magical fires within its splash area.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Limited"
>
Water Bomb (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 79

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 nonlethal bludgeoning damage and 4 nonlethal bludgeoning splash damage. Except on a critical failure, the bomb extinguishes nonmagical fires within its splash area.
name: Water Bomb

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Water Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1517)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Nonlethal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=188" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bladder of water explodes when put under pressure or upon being punctured, dealing minimal damage, neutralizing acids, and dousing flames. A water bomb deals the listed bludgeoning damage and bludgeoning [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage. On a hit, if the target is suffering from [persistent acid or persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), it can attempt a flat check to end that persistent damage immediately. As this is particularly effective assistance, the DC of the flat check is reduced from 15 to 10 for this check. On a hit against an unattended, non-magical fire, the bomb extinguishes the fire, or extinguishes one square of fire for a larger fire. Many types grant an item bonus to attack rolls, and some types extinguish wider areas of fire.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame **Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 79 **Price** 15 gp --- You dip thorns, spines, or pine needles in a mild toxin that causes skin irritation and swelling, then lash them together into a ball. When a creature enters the snare's square, the thorny ball is lobbed at that creature, dealing 3d8 piercing damage. The creature must attempt a DC 19 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage and 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and 2d6 persistent poison damage.
name: Irritating Thorn Snare

[Malleable Clay](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1522)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 80

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You Strike with the affixed weapon

**Requirement** You're trained in the affixed weapon.
</row>

</column>

---

This small lump of clay is malleable and can be molded into innumerable shapes and forms. When affixed, the clay takes on the appearance of the affixed weapon. When activated, the affixed weapon gains the [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) bludgeoning, versatile piercing, and versatile slashing weapon traits for the triggering Strike and all other attacks for 1 minute. With each attack, the clay changes shape, taking on the appearance of a different weapon that deals damage of the chosen damage type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Malleable Clay
requirement_markdown: You're trained in the affixed weapon.

This decorative silver disk is inscribed with magical runes, similar in appearance to a miniature teleportation circle but with the runes indicating movement encircled by runes associated with time. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Metuak's Pendant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1525)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 81

**Price** 155 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black quartz pendant carved in the shape of an [angel's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=3) feather hangs from a leather cord worn around the neck. Once worn by Metuak of the Burning Mammoths, this family heirloom anchors you to your ancestors, preserving your sense of self and protecting your mind from mental manipulation and demonic possession. You gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects that would make you [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=6), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). This bonus increases to +2 if the source of the effect is a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" />  ;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed at a saving throw against a mental effect that would make you confused, controlled, frightened, or stupefied;  **Effect** Empowered by your determination, your ancestors protect you and your nearby allies. For 1 minute, you and each of your allies within 30 feet gains a +1 status bonus to saving throws against mental effects that would make you confused, controlled, frightened, or stupefied.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Metuak's Pendant

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</column>

---

The opal set in this intricately carved ivory ring eventually becomes translucent and tickles your finger whenever an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creature is nearby. When in the presence of a nearby incorporeal creature, even if it's within a solid object, you eventually detect the creature, though you might not do so instantly, and you can't pinpoint the location. This acts as a [vague sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=413), similar to humans' sense of smell. An incorporeal creature trying to hide its presence from this sense attempts a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check against your Perception DC to hide from your vague sense, as normal for attempting to foil [special senses](/Rules.aspx?ID=414). You gain a +2 item bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) incorporeal creatures within 30 feet of you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spiritsight Ring

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Quest for the Frozen Flame</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #176: Lost Mammoth Valley](/Sources.aspx?ID=113) pg. 81, [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 146

**Price** 210 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sturdy leather boots have warm fur lining and magically augmented steel crampons that offer the wearer purchase on even the slipperiest ice slicks. _Sure-step crampons_ allow you to walk across ice without difficulty, ignoring the uneven ground and difficult terrain caused by ice, and reducing greater difficult terrain caused by ice to ordinary difficult terrain.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;    **Requirements** You're standing on an earthen, icy, or wooden surface; **Effect** You dig the crampons into the spot where you're standing, offering additional support until the next time you move. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against spells or effects that attempt to [move you](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The bonus lasts until you move from your current spot.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sure-Step Crampons

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 26"
    pfs=""
>
[Ancestral Embrace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1528)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 76

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This brightly colored _+4 [major resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_ is inscribed with a spiral that has no end or beginning. Though it is a symbol of the ancient religion of Holy Xatramba, it also serves as a [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=41) of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14). When you invest the armor, you gain negative resistance 25 and can draw on the power of your ancestors. You gain an ancestry feat from your ancestry; the feat can be any level, but you must meet any other prerequisites of the feat. You lose the feat when the investment ends, and you can select a different feat each time you invest the armor again.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You use an ancestry feat that has a limited number of uses per day;  **Effect** You gain an additional use of the ancestry feat. If you do not use it before the next time you make your daily preparations, the use is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;   **Trigger** You begin casting a [_legend lore_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=15) ritual to learn legends about your ancestry or about someone of your ancestry;  **Effect** _Ancestral Embrace_ reduces the casting time of this ritual to 1 hour, and you don't need secondary casters for this ritual.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor casts [_avatar_](/Spells.aspx?ID=16), granting these specific additional abilities instead of those associated with a deity: Speed 70 feet, [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), ignore difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain; **Melee** <actions string="Single Action" /> spear ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [thrown 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 6d6+6 piercing; **Ranged** <actions string="Single Action" /> blowgun ([range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus 5d6 poison.

**Destruction** _Ancestral Embrace_ crumbles if its wearer intentionally and knowingly kills or desecrates the remains of six generations of their family.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ancestral Embrace

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands **Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 77 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This shekere features carved prayers barely visible under a fine silk net of colorful beads. The handheld percussion instrument grants a +3 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks while playing music with it. In addition, when you [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59) with it, you can make it heard by any number of creatures within 100 miles. You can specify one or more specific creatures, or otherwise describe those who will hear it, such as all [humans](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6), or all inhabitants of a village. You can send a message through the music that the targets understand. [Demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) and creatures connected to demons (such as a creature whose deity is a [demon lord](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=5) or a [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=11) with the [demonic bloodline](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=3)) can't easily understand any messages conveyed in this way, even if you wish to send the message to them. They must attempt a [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) check against your Performance DC or against DC 40, whichever is higher. If your Performance DC is higher than DC 40 and you want demons to be able to understand the message, you can choose to use DC 40 instead. **Success** They understand the message.
**Failure** They don't understand the message but are aware there was one.
**Critical Failure** They don't realize the sound of the _halcyon heart_ contains a message. **Activate** command, Interact; **Requirements** The _halcyon heart_ has its net of beads; **Effect** You pull the net off the _halcyon heart_ and throw it at a creature within 20 feet, using your attack bonus for a ranged simple weapon. On a hit, the net grows and envelops the creature, which becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79), and on a critical hit, it's also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it Escapes. The net also attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effects and planar travel of the creature it's entrapping. The Escape DC is 44, and the net has a counteract level of 9 and a counteract modifier of +38. If the target is a demon or connected to a demon, they must roll twice and take the lower result on all attempts to Escape (this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect), and for counteract checks, the net rolls twice and takes the higher result (this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect). You can't Perform with the _halcyon heart_ while it doesn't have its net. So long as the net doesn't contain a creature, you can restore it with a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. **Destruction** If the Gorilla King plays a _halcyon heart_ in front of [Angazhan's](/Deities.aspx?ID=72) totem, the gourd decomposes, the net frays, and the beads shatter.
name: Halcyon Heart

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs=""
>
[Shadowed Scale, the Jungle Secret](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1530)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #174: Shadows of the Ancients](/Sources.aspx?ID=115) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gold-inlaid wooden mask depicts the reptilian visage of a [mokele-mbembe](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1230), a jungle predator seen by the Mwangi as embodiments of nature's strength and majesty. If you're a Magaambyan who already has a mask, you can use _Shadowed Scale, the Jungle Secret_ in lieu of your own mask for the purposes of mask-related abilities, such as [Magic Warrior Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=903). When you wear the mask, you are always [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while in a jungle.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;    **Requirements** Your last action was a melee Strike that killed a living creature or destroyed an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature; **Effect** You cause the body to decompose into a pile of fertile soil. The creature's body is completely destroyed at the start of your next turn, and it can only be returned to life with the use of powerful magic such as [_miracle_](/Spells.aspx?ID=196) or [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You are adjacent to the body of a creature you killed that has decomposed into soil; **Effect** You pull a seed from the mask and throw it into the soil, where it sprouts. You cast [_summon plant or fungus_](/Spells.aspx?ID=325); the spell is heightened to a level equal to one-half of the slain creature's level, rounded up. When the spell's duration ends, the plant creature returns to the soil, where it roots itself and becomes a non-creature piece of flora.

**Destruction** If the progenitor of all mokele-mbembes stomps on the mask, it is crushed to splinters.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadowed Scale, the Jungle Secret

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy, 12-foot-tall _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)_ is fashioned from mammoth tusks lashed together. Due to its great size, you can attack with the _Mammoth Bow_ only if you're Large or larger. The _Mammoth Bow_ deals 2d6 additional piercing damage because of its incredible, magically enhanced draw power. Additionally, the Mammoth Bow has a 180-foot [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) instead of a 100-foot range increment, but its [volley](/Traits.aspx?ID=201) range increases from 30 feet to 50 feet.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mirrored bottle contains various chemicals dissolved in two immiscible solvents. Shaking the bottle induces chemical reactions that cause it to glow. For 1 hour, the bottle sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) to the next 40 feet).

 During this hour, it can be thrown like a bomb. This requires a single action to Activate with a Strike, like other bombs, and deals the damage below. If it's not thrown within an hour of the first activation, it defuses harmlessly and is consumed.

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 2"

>
Bottled Sunlight (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d4 positive damage and 1 positive [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage, as well as 1d4 additional fire damage. As normal, positive damage harms only [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and other creatures with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42). Undead with a particular vulnerability to sunlight (such as [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) or [wraiths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=164)) take the maximum amount of positive damage from bottled sunlight (4 instead of 1d4). For any ability that depends on the number of weapon dice bottled sunlight has, count only the positive damage dice.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bottled Sunlight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1532)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mirrored bottle contains various chemicals dissolved in two immiscible solvents. Shaking the bottle induces chemical reactions that cause it to glow. For 1 hour, the bottle sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) to the next 40 feet).

 During this hour, it can be thrown like a bomb. This requires a single action to Activate with a Strike, like other bombs, and deals the damage below. If it's not thrown within an hour of the first activation, it defuses harmlessly and is consumed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d4 positive damage and 1 positive [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage, as well as 1d4 additional fire damage. As normal, positive damage harms only [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and other creatures with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42). Undead with a particular vulnerability to sunlight (such as [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) or [wraiths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=164)) take the maximum amount of positive damage from bottled sunlight (4 instead of 1d4). For any ability that depends on the number of weapon dice bottled sunlight has, count only the positive damage dice.

---

The bomb deals 4d4 positive damage, 4 positive splash damage, and 4d4 additional fire damage.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Celestial Peachwood Sword](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1533)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18, [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 128

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

From blade to pommel, this sword is carved from a branch of the now-extinct celestial peach tree. The blade of this _+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [disrupting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=294) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=299) [peachwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1543) [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43)_ has ancient runes that can permanently destroy the most powerful [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160)—if you are willing to pay the price. An undead creature of 12th level or lower that takes damage from a Strike with the sword takes 10d6 positive damage (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=326) Fortitude save). This damage is separate from the Strike itself and isn't included in any effect based on the Strike's damage.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;   **Trigger** You critically hit an undead creature with the _celestial peachwood sword_; **Requirements** once per day; **Effect** The creature must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude save or be destroyed. If the undead fails its saving throw and is destroyed, you suffer a backlash, taking 1d6 negative damage per level of the destroyed undead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Celestial Peachwood Sword

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dawnlight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1534)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This shining symbol of [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16) depicts the goddess with her arms held wide. In the center of the symbol is a crystal reliquary with a perfect feather floating inside, glowing bright as a candle with the light of the goddess.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day for each spell;   **Effect** The dawnlight casts 1st-level [_disrupt undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=79) or [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171). The _dawnlight's_ spell attack roll and [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier are +7, and any spell with the [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) trait is treated as though its counteract level were 1 higher for counteracting [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39).

**Craft Requirements** You have a spellcasting class feature with the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) tradition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dawnlight
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dawnlight (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18

**Price** 320 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Whenever you successfully counteract magical darkness while holding this symbol, the clash of magic unleashes a momentary flash of light. Creatures with [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) in the area of the _light_ spell or targeted by it must succeed at a DC 23 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round. Those with [light blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22) are also exposed to [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407).

 Change the list of spells to 3rd-level versions of _disrupt undead_, _light_, and [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269). The spell attack roll and counteract modifier are +13.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dawnlight (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A _major dawnlight_ creates a flash of light like a _greater dawnlight_, but the DC is 28. Change the list of spells to  5th-level versions of _disrupt undead_ and _light_, and 4th-level versions of _searing light_ and [_vital beacon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=359). The spell attack roll and counteract modifier are +18.
name: Dawnlight

Those with [light blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22) are also exposed to [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407).

 Change the list of spells to 3rd-level versions of _disrupt undead_, _light_, and [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269). The spell attack roll and counteract modifier are +13.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ectoplasmic Tracer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1535)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 19

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sticky, fluorescing substance, stored in an atomizing nozzle, sprays all creatures within a 15-foot cone when released. This marks any [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures in the cone for 1d4 days. Anyone attempting to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=66) a marked creature gains a +2 item bonus to the check. The tracer has no effect on corporeal creatures, nor incorporeal creatures not formed of spiritual essence, such as [animate dreams](/Monsters.aspx?ID=546) that are purely mental in nature.

 A marked creature that's aware of the tracer can attempt to remove it, but the process is lengthy and unpleasant. It takes 10 minutes of scraping through its incorporeal form to remove the tracer's effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ectoplasmic Tracer

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Feast of Hungry Ghosts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1536)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 19

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>1 hour (Interact)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lavish meal with meats, fresh and dried fruits, grains, and wine smells absolutely scrumptious, especially to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). It comes complete with dishes and dining utensils. You spend an hour setting up this feast to feed one undead creature, who is present throughout the process. The undead must be willing, but the food smells delicious and feeds any unusual hunger the undead has, so an undead motivated mainly by hunger will usually be willing to dine. [Incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) undead consume the various essences of the meal, allowing them to eat it despite their lack of a body. After it has consumed the meal, the undead becomes [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18) to you for 24 hours, or until you take actions to antagonize or anger it. The meal also sates the undead during that time, which could allow an undead with an unnatural hunger to stave off that hunger for a time. 

If you eat or drink a morsel of the leftover meal after the undead has eaten its fill, you can communicate with the undead as if you shared a language. Though you can feed a meal to a [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) undead, it does not give the mindless undead or you the ability to communicate with one another.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 19

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 3

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Banquet of Hungry Ghosts
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 19

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 8

</row>

</column>

---

This feast feeds up to 4 willing undead.
name: Feast of Hungry Ghosts

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 19 **Price** 24,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31) --- This _+3 [greater disrupting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=294) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_ is made from the purest silver. The blade is carefully etched to depict a vast and sprawling necropolis, a place where the dead are laid to rest. Whenever you critically hit an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature with _final rest_, the undead creature takes 3d6 [persistent good damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), with a DC 36 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** No effect.
**Success** The creature takes half the persistent good damage.
**Failure** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) and takes the full persistent good damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 3 and takes double the persistent good damage. **Activate** command, envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You hold the sword aloft, making it cast [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=326) (DC 38 Reflex save). **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 4,950 gp of [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277).

---

This golden ring is sculpted with the visage of a grinning skull on the side. While wearing the ring, you can attempt to detect the presence of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures as an [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=66) activity. This reveals the presence or absence of undead in the area, but it doesn't pinpoint their locations. This ring can't detect undead whose appearance is masked by any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92) spell that is 2nd level or higher. If an undead is hiding or disguised, the GM rolls a secret Perception check for you against the undead's [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) or [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC, as appropriate, with a +2 item bonus to your check.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You gain the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) condition from an undead creature;  **Effect** Reduce the value of the drained condition you gain by 1, and the undead that caused the condition takes 2d6 positive damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This _+1 [disrupting](/Equipment.aspx?ID=294) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)_ has a single strip of anointed leather wrapped around its hilt in a spiral pattern. The handle of the whip ends in an oak spike that has been sanctified with ashes from [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15). If the spike is used to stake a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) that's vulnerable to being staked, the vampire is immediately destroyed, without having to sever its head and anoint it with [_holy water_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=245). If the whip is buried with a creature, that creature can't rise as an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) as long as the whip remains by its side.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />

This crystal vial contains salts blessed by channeling life-giving energies into waters purified by holy fire. You can activate this vial by throwing it as a Strike. It's a simple [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) ranged weapon with a range increment of 10 feet. Unlike an alchemical bomb, it doesn't add the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait to the attack made with it. On a hit against an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160), life salt deals 1 [persistent positive damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and the undead must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be unable to attack you as long as it continues taking the persistent positive damage. If you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against any undead, this second effect ends, and the undead can attack you normally.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

Symbols of good fortune and luck are carved on this thin, square wooden plaque. It smells of sandalwood from the blessings placed upon it. You aren't [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) to [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39), or flanking undead of your level or lower, or undead of your level or lower using surprise attack. **Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 20 **Price** 20 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- The liquid in this test tube is as clear as water. You can drop in a sample collected from the environment or a creature to reveal what kind of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) has been in contact with the sample in the past 24 hours. The water changes color, as seen on the table, or remains clear if it doesn't detect any traces of undead. The higher the undead's level or number of undead in contact with the sample, the more intense the color. This isn't a foolproof way to identify a disguised creature as undead, since any contact with undead causes the sample to change colors. If an undead has been disguising its undead nature or its presence in an area, the GM can roll a secret DC 20 [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check for the creature when the dye is activated. On a success, the creature avoided leaving traces. This can't protect the undead from discovery if it actively uses its undead abilities on an area or creature, though it can attempt to remove any evidence with activities like [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=65) and [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=61). ## Undead Detection Dye
**Color****Undead Detected**
Red[Vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97)
Orange[Wight](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=163)
Yellow[Mummy](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=74)
Green[Ghost](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51)
Blue[Ghoul](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=52)
Indigo[Wraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=164)
Purple[Shadow](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=89)
Gray[Mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) undead such as [zombie](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=103) or [skeleton](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=92)
Black[Lich](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=68)
WhiteOther undead
name: Undead Detection Dye

    level="1"
    right="Item 12"

>
[Soul Cage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1545)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 51, [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 219

**Price** 1,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

As you [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) your _soul cage_, you trap your soul within it, an integral part of the complicated process of becoming a [lich](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=68). When you're destroyed, your soul flees to the _soul cage_, which rebuilds your undead body over the course of 1d10 days.

 Afterward, you manifest next to the _soul cage_, fully healed and in a new body (lacking any equipment you had on your old body). Only destroying your _soul cage_ can prevent you from returning. You choose the form of your _soul cage_ when you Craft it. A few options include a miniature cage, a ring, an amulet, or a crown. Work with your GM to adjust the usage and Bulk as needed, though a _soul cage_ is rarely more than light Bulk. The _soul cage_ has Hardness 9 and 36 HP.

 If your _soul cage_ is destroyed but you aren't, you can attempt to find your soul and trap it again, building a new _soul cage_. This is no trivial feat and often takes an entire adventure to accomplish. If you don't cage your soul again, you suffer a long decline as described on page 119.

**Craft Requirements** You have [Lich Dedication.](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=179)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Soul Cage

<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Activating this black powder–infused alchemical bandage cauterizes wounds. You can apply a searing suture to yourself or an adjacent willing creature as you activate it. The target creature takes 1d6 fire damage and can immediately attempt a flat check to remove the [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)—the DC of this flat check depends on the type of searing suture applied. When applied against a bleed effect that is lower level than the searing suture, the flat check is automatically successful.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[War Blood Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1549)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Outlaws of Alkenstar</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 77

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Upon drinking this mutagen, you can feel your blood surge through your body and hear a low-pitched humming in your ears. These sensations subside as the flesh and muscles of your arm loosen and stretch, the fibers of your very being reaching out to combine with the base of one melee weapon you're holding.

 **Benefit** Choose one melee weapon you're holding in one or both hands. You gain the listed item bonus to your attack rolls with this weapon and to Will saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects. Any attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) or otherwise remove the weapon from your hands must exceed the listed DC based on the type of war blood mutagen or the attempt fails.

 **Drawback** You can't voluntarily remove the weapon unless you succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check at the listed DC, which ends the war blood mutagen's effects; this check takes a single action. If you perform an action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is lost.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Limited"
>
War Blood Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #178: Punks in a Powderkeg](/Sources.aspx?ID=119) pg. 77

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +1, the DC to remove the weapon is 25, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
War Blood Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, the DC to remove the weapon is 30, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Limited"
>
War Blood Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

 **Benefit** Choose one melee weapon you're holding in one or both hands. You gain the listed item bonus to your attack rolls with this weapon and to Will saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects. Any attempt to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) or otherwise remove the weapon from your hands must exceed the listed DC based on the type of war blood mutagen or the attempt fails.

 **Drawback** You can't voluntarily remove the weapon unless you succeed at an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check at the listed DC, which ends the war blood mutagen's effects; this check takes a single action. If you perform an action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, you must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is lost.

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Horn of the Sun Aurochs](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1550)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Shadows at Sundown</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shadows at Sundown](/Sources.aspx?ID=121) pg. 53

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A horn of the sun aurochs grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks when played as an instrument.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The horn is clearly audible for hundreds of feet, but within a 20-foot emanation, the sound causes agony in those who wish harm upon the user; the horn also sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in that area and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) to the next 20 feet. Enemies of the user in this area take 7d6 sonic damage and 7d6 good damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) DC 30 Fortitude save). Creatures that are specifically vulnerable to sunlight are also [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) if they fail this save or frightened 2 on a critical failure. This activation also attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=39) or [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) effects in the area (+23 counteract check).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Horn of the Sun Aurochs

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Bulk** L --- You can purchase special armor for animals, called barding (shown on Table 6–18). All animals have a trained proficiency rank in light barding, and combat-trained animals are trained in heavy barding. Barding uses the same rules as armor except for the following. The Price and Bulk of barding depend on the animal’s size. Unlike for a suit of armor, barding’s Strength entry is listed as a modifier, not a score. Barding can’t be etched with magic runes, though special magical barding might be available. ## Table 6-18: Barding
**Size****Barding****Price****AC Bonus****Dex Cap****Check Penalty****Speed Penalty****Bulk****Strength**
LightSmall or Medium10 gp+1+5-1-5 ft.2+3
Large20 gp+1+5-1-5 ft.4+3
HeavySmall or Medium (Level 2)25 gp+3+3-3-10 ft.4+5
Large (Level 3)50 gp+3+3-3-10 ft.8+5
name: Barding

A hollowed hilt is a weapon modification that allows a pommel to be detached and its hilt meticulously hollowed out to create a small compartment without greatly affecting its balance. Often used by knight emissaries to hide important missives, this space can hold an object of light Bulk about the size of a scroll. These cloaks are designed to accommodate long treks through various climates. Traveler's cloaks for hot climates might be bleached white and created from lighter materials, while those intended for cold climates are made of thicker materials and have linings intended to retain heat. While wearing a traveler's cloak for the appropriate type of weather, increase the time it takes to become [fatigued from temperature effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) by 2 hours. A traveler's cloak has no effect at extreme or incredible temperatures.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 87

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to medium or heavy armor

</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Parade armor is an armor adjustment that creates a set of pseudo-decorative armor to wear during noncombat functions. Any medium or heavy armor can be fashioned into parade armor, which features custom designs based on the wearer's affiliation, military branch, or rank. A particular faction typically has one uniform appearance for its set of parade armor. While wearing this armor, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against creatures of the same affiliation as your parade armor, though the bonus to Intimidation doesn't apply when interacting with creatures of higher rank than you. The armor is slightly bulkier, increasing the Bulk by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Parade Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Throwing Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1561)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Adjustment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=380" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 87

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** applied to a buckler or shield

</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These special straps and weightings fit over a shield or buckler, but not a tower shield. They are designed to let you doff and throw the shield in one swift movement. You can quickly remove the shield by spending a free action as part of the Strike to throw the shield. When thrown in this way, the shield is a martial thrown weapon that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage and has the [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195) trait. A shield with the throwing shield attachment can't have any attached weapons, such as [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42) or a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41), and the adjustments to make it more aerodynamic make it slightly less sturdy, reducing its Hardness by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Throwing Shield

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 88

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Based on the armors used by the holy warriors who served under General Arnisant in the Shining Crusade, this well-polished _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12)_ is decorated with religious motifs and carved with sacred scripture. While wearing _armor of the holy warrior_, your item bonus from the _greater resilient_ rune increases from +2 to +3; if you upgrade to a _major resilient_ rune, the item bonus instead increases from +3 to +4. An [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) that critically fails an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack against you while you wear this armor takes 1d6 positive damage.

 If you are evil, you're [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) while wearing the armor. If you're undead, you also take 2d6 positive damage each round that you wear the armor.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor casts the three-action version of a 6th-level [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell.

</column>
name: Armor of the Holy Warrior

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 88

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carried by many Knights of Lastwall, this amber-colored, fragrant-smelling oil is meant to prevent those who fall in battle from rising as [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). Applying anointing oil to a corpse casts [_gentle repose_](/Spells.aspx?ID=131) on it. The effects last for 24 hours. The oil is repugnant to the undead. An undead creature that touches a corpse treated with this oil is [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) until the contact is broken or the oil's effect wears off.

</column>

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 88 **Price** 700 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item. --- Carried by knights who expect to spend the night in the open, this simple-looking banner is accompanied by a long cross pole from which it hangs. Usually, the banner appears to be a piece of plain cloth; when activated, it automatically adopts whatever insignia the person activating it desires. **Activate** 10 minutes (command, envision, Interact); **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You plant the banner firmly in the ground, and a comfortable environment is created around it in a 20-foot radius. Those within the area are shielded against your choice of hot or cold temperatures, gaining the effects of 1st-level [_endure elements_](/Spells.aspx?ID=99). The area created by the banner lasts for 8 hours or until it is removed from the ground.
As part of the activation, you can choose to ward the same area with an [_alarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=7) spell. In addition to a mental alert for the person who Activates the banner, the alert sent by the alarm spell also includes an audible alarm with the sound and volume of a hand bell, which has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) trait. You choose the password as you decide to ward the area.
name: Banner of the Restful

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Barding Saddle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1565)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Companion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=29" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 88

**Price** 330 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn saddle

**Bulk** 4
</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This saddle is covered with well-polished metal plates on the outside and adjusts itself to fit any mount.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact;     **Effect** You touch the metal plates of the saddle, and they begin to unfold and adapt to the creature, covering your mount in [heavy barding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1552). This leaves the mount covered with the barding and wearing a simple-looking saddle. The bulk of the saddle is unchanged in either form, but your mount is not affected by the restrictions, nor the benefits, of wearing barding while in saddle form. If the mount is already wearing any barding, this has no effect. You can make the barding return to its saddle form by following the same process.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Barding Saddle
