<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Busine of Divine Reinforcement](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1566)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 89

**Price** 6,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, straight, trumpet-like instrument is made of well-polished brass and adorned with imagery of angels fighting demons or religious symbols. When played as an instrument, the busine generates powerful and harmonious notes and grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You play a few notes on the busine. Evil creatures within 60 feet who hear the notes must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) for 1 round, or 2 rounds on a critical failure; this is an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) effect.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You blow into the busine and conjure a [celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16) with the effects of a 6th-level [_summon celestial_](/Spells.aspx?ID=317) spell, but there are no alignment restrictions to define which celestials you can call. The celestial will fight only evil creatures and leaves after all present evil creatures have been defeated or the spell's duration ends, whichever comes first. You can Sustain the Activation to extend the spell's duration, as normal. If you have a deity and the conjured celestial's alignment is one of your deity's preferred alignments, the effect functions as a normal 6th-level _summon celestial_ spell instead, meaning the celestial will fight any creature, not just evil creatures, and will perform other tasks as you command.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Busine of Divine Reinforcement

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Called (Weapon Rune)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1567)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 89

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _called_ weapon can be teleported to its owner's hand.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (command, envision);     **Effect** You imprint upon the weapon, proclaiming yourself as its owner. This allows you to use the rune's second activation until someone else uses this activation or succeeds at a Will save to prevent you from using the second activation.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;     **Effect** You extend your hand and call the weapon. If the weapon is within 100 feet of you, it appears in your hand even if it was restrained. If the weapon is in another creature's possession, that creature can attempt a Will save against your Will DC. If the creature succeeds, your bond as the owner is broken, and you can't use this activation again until you use the first activation to restore the connection.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Called (Weapon Rune)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Camp Shroud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1568)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 89

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Knights make use of this magically treated powder to help hide their camps while traveling through the Gravelands or other dangerous regions. When you toss the powder into a campfire or other sizable fire, the fire produces a thin mist that enshrouds everything in a 10-foot emanation from the fire. The mist creates protective illusions that remain for up to 12 hours and make it difficult to spot the area from afar. You can end the effect earlier by putting out the fire. Light and smoke produced in the area aren't visible from outside the area. The illusions don't prevent sound from traveling nor prevent the area or its inhabitants from being seen.

 A creature outside the area that uses the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action or [Search](/Actions.aspx?ID=519) activity to examine the area or look for smoke and light coming from that direction can attempt a DC 18 Perception check to disbelieve the illusion.

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 89 **Price** 500 gp **Bulk** L when not activated **Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item. --- Used to facilitate the care of mounts during long campaigns or when it's necessary to move camp frequently, this object appears to be a simple, worn wooden horseshoe. **Activate** 1 minute (command, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You place the horseshoe on the ground and it unfolds into a spacious stable with a wide central aisle, stalls to accommodate up to eight horses, and sufficient feed and water to sustain the horses for 1 day. The stalls can house other quadrupedal mounts, but the stable provides only horse feed, which might be unsuitable for other creatures. As a 1-minute activity, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, you can alter the shape of the stalls to accommodate different mounts, though the space available within the stable never changes, only the layout.
A metal horseshoe hangs inside the stable near the entrance. You can remove the horseshoe with an Interact action, which causes the stable to fold into the horseshoe, leaving it again looking as if it's made of worn wood.
name: Campaign Stable

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Detector Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1570)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 90

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

During their journeys across the Gravelands, the Knights of Lastwall found several smoky, gemstone-like objects that knights refer to as _detector stones_. Some of the knights' Magaambyan allies believe the stones are [_aeon stones_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=407) corrupted by the same energies that corrupted the Gravelands, though they don't display the characteristic orbiting behavior of _aeon stones_ when invested. Despite the controversy, many knights use the stones for their ability to detect the presence of [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160).

 While you have a _detector stone_ invested, it glows faintly when an undead creature is within 30 feet of you, unless the undead is hidden or disguised with a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) result of 20 or higher. An invested stone may be carried in a pocket, but some knights set them in necklaces, bracelets, or weapon hilts.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You channel your life-force into the stone, empowering it temporarily. The stone can detect undead within 60 feet of you, rather than 30 feet, until the end of your turn. Undead hiding or disguising themselves must make an additional Deception or Stealth check to remain disguised or hidden from the activation, and the DC of the check is 23 rather than 20.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** one per hour;   **Effect** You focus on the stone as it glows, and your eyes glow with the same tainted light. You detect the auras of evil undead in a 30-foot radius around you and learn each aura's location and strength. This has the effects of a 2nd-level [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) spell but only detects evil undead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Detector Stone

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghost Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1571)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 90

**Price** 18 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The vials containing this translucent, unsubstantial oil are always cold to the touch. Applying ghost oil to a melee weapon you're wielding or carrying causes it to become semitransparent and gain the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) rune, but it also makes the weapon unable to harm corporeal creatures. The oil has no effect when applied to another creature's weapon. The effect of the oil lasts for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Oil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Guardian Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1572)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 90

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this item.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A guardian shield is a [steel shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 9, HP 36, BT 18) engraved with the symbol of a good deity.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You conjure a Medium guardian with the effects of the [_spiritual guardian_](/Spells.aspx?ID=305) spell, but it carries a shield instead of weapons and can't attack. The guardian appears in an unoccupied space next to an ally of your choice within range and uses the [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98) action, granting that ally a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the beginning of your next turn or until your ally is no longer adjacent to the _spiritual guardian_. Each time you Sustain the Activation, the guardian moves to the ally, as long as the ally remains within range, and Raises its Shield, granting its bonus again until the beginning of your next turn or until your ally is no longer adjacent to the _spiritual guardian_. When the ally receiving the AC bonus takes damage beyond the 10 that's redirected to the guardian, you can use a reaction to have the guardian reduce damage for your ally even more by interposing itself further. When it blocks damage in this way, it reduces the damage your ally takes by an additional 20 (for a total of 30 damage reduced), but Dismisses the guardian, immediately ending the activation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Guardian Shield
